formal_strategy: ONE_SHOT_DONCHIAN_ATR_BREAKOUT 1.0.1 on BTCUSDT-PERP; comparison background only
question: Can a small predeclared family of literature-derived daily Donchian channel ensembles on Binance USD-M BTCUSDT perpetual remain profitable after actual funding, market-order costs and sequential time gates, while fitting a single-owner product risk cap?
primary_claim: At least one fixed daily Donchian ensemble has positive base and stress net performance in development, evaluation and confirmation, improves risk-adjusted performance over a volatility-matched continuous long benchmark, and is stable across nearby published horizon groups.

signal_and_execution.entry: long when close equals the n-day rolling maximum; symmetric short candidates enter when close equals the n-day rolling minimum
signal_and_execution.exit: long exits when close is at or below a non-decreasing Donchian midpoint trailing stop; short exits symmetrically at or above a non-increasing midpoint trailing stop
signal_and_execution.action_time: signal and desired weight fixed after day t close; trade at day t+1 open
signal_and_execution.volatility: 90 completed close-to-close returns, annualized with sqrt(365)
signal_and_execution.rebalance_threshold: entry, exit or sign change always trades; otherwise rebalance only when absolute target-current weight exceeds 20% of max(abs(target), abs(current)); zero-to-zero does not trade
signal_and_execution.funding: all actual Binance funding events; positive funding debits longs and credits shorts using event mark price when present; events within 60 seconds after the daily open apply to the carried pre-rebalance quantity, which is conservative for a close-triggered order submitted around the funding boundary
signal_and_execution.accounting: linear perpetual quantity and USDT equity, marked from one daily open to the next

diagnostics_not_eligible_for_selection: published LONG_FULL_9 at 25% annual volatility target and 2.0x cap
continuous volatility-targeted long with the candidate 10% target and 0.5x cap
individual published lookback components for neighborhood and concentration diagnostics
development_gate: data PASS; base and stress total return > 0; base Sharpe > 0.30; base maximum drawdown > -20%; at least two of 2021/2022/2023 base returns > 0; base Sharpe and Calmar both exceed the volatility-matched continuous-long benchmark; component concentration leaves at least two lookback groups with positive base return; DSR probability computed across the six eligible trials >= 0.80; rank passers by worst annual base return, stress Sharpe, base Sharpe, then fewer lookbacks and candidate id; keep at most two
evaluation_gate: evaluate only development-selected candidates; base and stress total return > 0; base maximum drawdown > -18%; both 2024 and 2025 are not below -8% and at least one is positive; base Sharpe and Calmar both exceed the same benchmark; first development-ranked passer only
confirmation_gate: evaluate only the single evaluation passer; base and stress total return >= 0; base maximum drawdown > -12%; combined evaluation plus confirmation base and stress total return > 0; no execution, funding or data-quality exception
conclusion_rule: SUPPORTS_WITHIN_SCOPE only if all sequential gates pass and independent replay matches. A non-positive development/evaluation/confirmation net result or failure of an economic gate is DOES_NOT_SUPPORT for this fixed family. A data, replay or effective-sample limitation that prevents a valid economic decision is INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE.
robustness_if_authorized: full candidate distribution and annual/subperiod returns
20% rebalance band versus trade-every-day diagnostic without changing selection
one-day execution lag and daily intrabar adverse-path checks
independent replay from immutable archive hashes
known_exposure: The underlying 2021-2026H1 BTCUSDT market history has already been viewed in other Halpha studies. This study therefore provides a predeclared rule-level sequential holdout, not untouched market data. The exact daily midpoint-trailing ensemble family has not previously been run in the repository. Genuine new-market evidence can only come from later forward Demo observation.
allowed_fixes_after_checkpoint: download, checksum, parsing, timestamp and data-quality defects
implementation defects that do not alter candidates, signals, timing, sizing, costs, funding, periods, ranks or gates
adding explanatory text or diagnostics that cannot affect candidate selection
forbidden_after_checkpoint: adding or removing candidates or lookbacks after viewing results
changing target volatility, cap, rebalance threshold, costs, periods, ranks or gates
adding ATR, volume, funding-sign, regime or discretionary filters
reading evaluation before development selection authorizes it
reading confirmation before evaluation authorizes it
using paper-scale leverage to qualify a product candidate
external_anchor: Zarattini, Pagani and Barbon (2025), Catching Crypto Trends; A Tactical Approach for Bitcoin and Altcoins, Swiss Finance Institute Research Paper 25-80
framework: vectorbt 1.1.0 for return statistics and selection-bias metrics; deterministic NumPy/Pandas state simulation for funding-aware linear-perpetual accounting
pre_result_data_revision: Before any candidate return was calculated, the first funding quality load found 4,198 historical funding records without markPrice. The source manifest and loader were extended with official Binance 8h mark-price klines. They backfill 4,173 events; 25 events at six official mark-price timeline gaps conservatively use that UTC day's perpetual open. Candidate definitions, periods, costs, ranks and gates did not change.
product_effects: NONE until all gates pass, an owner-authorized product handoff is implemented, and Demo-only validation is explicitly created with creator_kind AI
